examples: The Europeans, beset by difficulties in the eurozone, are desperate that the job should once again go to one of them.
The novels chronicle the experiences of women who, beset with hardships, find within themselves qualities of intelligence, will, resourcefulness, and courage sufficient to overcome their obstacles.
The team was beset by injury all season.
Construction was beset by strikes and it staged the games in an unfinished form.
Beset by mischance, confounded by disorder, they had mostly done what they were asked to do.
